raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "A California lawmaker has introduced legislation that would bar colleges and universities from punishing students who call 911 for help during a drug overdose.",
    "Currently, students who are found to be in the presence of drugs can be expelled or suspended, which strongly discourages them from calling 911.",
    "AB 602 aims to combat rising deaths from drugs contaminated with fentanyl by encouraging students to seek help from medical authorities immediately.",
    "More than 7,000 Californians died from overdoses in 2022, according to state data.",
    "California’s Good Samaritan law protects individuals from criminal charges when seeking help during an overdose but does not shield students from academic penalties.",
    "Fear of university discipline can deter students from calling 911 during overdose emergencies.",
    "At UC Berkeley, students can face disciplinary actions even after surviving an overdose.",
    "The bill would require colleges to establish formal policies protecting students in overdose situations.",
    "The bill would mandate access to harm-reduction resources for students rather than imposing disciplinary measures.",
    "Advocates argue that the proposal could save lives by eliminating a major deterrent to emergency intervention."
  ]
}
```
